steps.title: Identify the chemical signature the test produced
steps.content: The passage draws a clear contrast: mineral-based ocher gets its color from iron oxide, while plant-based pigments contain a high level of carbon. The 2023 test of the Natufian beads found no iron but a high level of carbon—the exact chemical fingerprint of a plant-based pigment, not ocher.

steps.title: Match the chemical evidence to each conclusion
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘the beads are the oldest surviving examples of plant-based pigment use’: The test shows what kind of pigment was used; it says nothing about whether older examples exist elsewhere. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the Natufian beadmakers used plant-based pigments rather than ocher’: No iron rules out ocher; high carbon confirms a plant-based source. This follows directly from the chemical data. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘the beadmakers preferred plant-based pigments because they are brighter’: The passage never compares brightness of pigment types. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘ocher was difficult to find’: The test reveals pigment chemistry, not local availability of materials. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
